methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Theion is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Ulrich Ehmes that has raised €25M+. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 private peers and 1 public company (LNZA) operating in the same sector.

The peer-median method forms the basis of this analysis, given data availability at the Series A stage. Three directly comparable companies—Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy—all at Series A in climate and energy, carry valuations of $200M, $150M, and $110M respectively. The median across this set is $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. This peer-median approach implies a valuation of $150M for Theion.

The triangulated analysis, constrained by the single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $128M (low) to $172M (high), with a median point of $150M. The relatively narrow anchoring to peer-median valuation reflects limited data diversity—only one methodological approach proved tractable from the available inputs. Key uncertainties include Theion's relative progress on key technology milestones and capital efficiency relative to the three closest comps, neither of which is quantified in the current dataset. A fuller triangulation incorporating revenue multiples or comparable transaction multiples would strengthen confidence in this range.
